purpose: Maps the three-way constraint the Fed faces during a potential AI bust with above-target inflation to assess the structural impairment of the Fed put
components: Option 1: Intervene in Treasuries creates fiscal dominance concerns especially with basis trade unwinding
Option 2: Cut rates aggressively risks unanchoring inflation expectations starting at 2.5-3.5%
Option 3: Stand pat risks deleveraging cascade through cross-margined positions
Current inflation 2.5-3.5% limits easing to 150-250bp in 6 months vs 550bp in 2001
Historical easing/tightening asymmetry ratio averages 3.5x across 3 episodes
current_assessment: Fed AI put is structurally impaired vs 1998-2001. No clean analogue exists for an AI bust with 3%+ inflation. The 2020 precedent is misleading because Fed could print into deflationary shock. Basis trade adds novel dimension where Treasury intervention itself could be destabilizing.
limitations: Trilemma assumes all three options equally constrained; Fed may find a hierarchy in practice
Inflation might decelerate during AI bust partially relaxing constraint
Historical asymmetry ratio may not apply given novel conditions
Does not account for coordination with other central banks or fiscal authorities
